key_insight: Adaptive routing in a diffusion-transformer model improves preoperative prediction of perineural invasion (PNI) by balancing global context modeling with computational efficiency.
clinical_relevance: Better MRI-based PNI prediction could help refine cholangiocarcinoma risk stratification before surgery.
method: Reformulated PNI prediction as a diffusion-based classification task.
Used a transformer-based denoising network to capture volumetric MRI context.
Added adaptive routing across attention heads, spatial tokens, and MLP width to reduce compute.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an AUC of 0.731 with 257.57 GFLOPs, indicating moderate predictive performance with substantial but managed computational cost.
watch_item: The abstract does not provide external validation or dataset details, so generalizability and real-world robustness remain uncertain.
paper_title: Adaptive Routing for Efficient Diffusion Transformer-Based PNI Prediction
